metadata.description: Modular research-agent skills: remote GPU jobs, adversarial claim verification. Extracted from a working autonomous-research lab.

plugins.name: gpu-job
plugins.description: Give your coding agent GPU powers: disposable cloud GPU pods with your environment restored, detached job execution, artifact persistence, verified teardown (never bill an idle GPU). Backend: RunPod.

plugins.name: verify-claims
plugins.description: Verification gates that catch the confidently-wrong-number class before money or conclusions move: deterministic full-pool data audits plus adversarial fact/design/data/evidence checks read by an independent model family (read-only sandboxed). Also --scaffold: cross-family DESIGN REVIEW of product/scaffold change proposals before they land.

plugins.name: experiment-lifecycle
plugins.description: Run GPU experiments like a researcher — the full lifecycle: design-experiment (pre-register a DESIGN.md, clear it with the human through cross-family validity gates, emit a START.md brief + CHECKLIST.md, dispatch to a fresh-context executor) and run-experiment (the zero-context executor: self-wake, acquire/provision/drive/collect/close). Instance specifics live in an execution profile; pairs with gpu-job and verify-claims.

plugins.name: aar-engineering
plugins.description: The SWE pipeline that builds the product itself: ship-change drives a scaffold change through a GitHub-backed lifecycle — Issue → worktree branch → namespaced design doc → draft PR → cross-family --scaffold design review → implement → cross-family --code review → mechanical/architectural classifier → tracked .aar-ci checks + behavior smoke → fail-closed merge-when-clean. The agents are the engineers; a change is authored by one model family and reviewed by the other. Phase 1 runs in shadow mode. Pairs with verify-claims and a per-repo .aar-ci/ profile.
